urgency.message: 65 critical CVE(s) and 6 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: In Other News: Chinese Mythos-Like AI, Tata Electronics Breach, Snyk Layoffs
items.summary:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Russia used Cellebrite to hack activist’s phone, Five Eyes issue urgent AI threat warning, macOS Gaslight backdoor, Scattered Spider guilty pleas.
The post In Other News: Chinese Mythos-Like AI, Tata Electronics Breach, Snyk Layoffs a

items.title: Your First GRC Agent: A Red Teamer's Walkthrough
items.summary: AI won't replace GRC analysts, but it can eliminate much of the repetitive work they do. Anecdotes walks through building an agent that continuously monitors controls, identifies evidence gaps, and opens remediation tasks. [...]

items.title: New Linux pedit COW Exploit Enables Root Access by Poisoning Cached Binaries
items.summary:
A flaw in the Linux kernel's traffic-control subsystem can let a local unprivileged user gain root on affected systems.

CVE-2026-46331, nicknamed "pedit COW," is an out-of-bounds write in the packet-editing action (act_pedit) that corrupts shared page-cache memory. A public, working exploit&nb

items.title: Amazon Q Developer Flaw Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code via MCP Configs
items.summary:
A high-severity flaw in Amazon Q Developer let a malicious repository run commands and steal a developer's cloud credentials. The path was short: a developer opens the repo, trusts the workspace, and Amazon Q does the rest. Amazon has patched it.

Tracked as CVE-2026-12957 (CVSS 8.5), the

items.title: Russia accuses Apple of ‘political censorship’ after VK apps removed from App Store
items.summary: Apple removed VK's flagship social network VKontakte, often described as Russia's equivalent of Facebook, along with VK Music, VK Messenger, VK Video, Odnoklassniki and Mail.ru services, including its email application.

items.title: Turla group adds more malware to Russia’s espionage efforts against Ukraine
items.summary: Threat intelligence researchers at Google described StockStay, the latest malware developed by the Russian cyber-espionage group known as Turla.

items.title: Meeting Trump's 2030 Quantum Deadline Will be Expensive, Complex
items.summary: Getting accurate visibility into IT and OT systems will be compounded by multivendor environments, misaligned update life cycles, and interoperability gaps.

items.title: Russia used social engineering to breach prominent messaging accounts, Ukraine says
items.summary: Ukraine's SBU described a long-running Russian operation that used fake tech-support workers to persuade people to hand over credentials to their messaging apps.

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 26
items.summary: Operation Endgame takes down Amadey and StealC servers, macOS.Gaslight floods AI triage with fake errors, and attackers exploit Cisco flaws for root access.

items.title: Thanks for Crushing the Submissions Inbox. We're Trying to Keep Up
items.summary: It might be taking a bit longer than usual to respond to your submissions — here's why.

items.title: Nebulock Raises $25 Million for AI-Native Contextual Security
items.summary:
The cybersecurity startup provides threat hunting, proactive detection, and behavioral security analytics.
The post Nebulock Raises $25 Million for AI-Native Contextual Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Adds Exploited PTC Windchill RCE Flaw to KEV as Web Shell Attacks Continue
items.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a critical remote code execution vulnerability impacting PTC Windchill PDMlink and PTC FlexPLM enterprise Product Data Management (PDM) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabil

items.title: FCC votes to toughen rules in bid to better protect undersea cables
items.summary: In an unprecedented move, the FCC also said it plans to mandate that owners and operators of submarine line terminal equipment (SLTE) be licensed.

items.title: New DirtyClone Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root via Cloned Packets
items.summary:
DirtyClone is a new Linux kernel privilege escalation in the DirtyFrag family. JFrog Security Research published a working exploit walkthrough for the flaw on June 25, the first public demonstration for this variant.

Tracked as CVE-2026-43503 (CVSS 8.8), it lets a local user cor

items.title: Guardian Agents: The Next Layer of Identity Governance
items.summary: AI agents are moving through enterprise environments, inheriting permissions, traversing systems, and executing decisions at machine speed with minimal oversight. The identity infrastructure built to govern human access wasn't designed for autonomous actors, and the gap between what enterprises are

items.title: Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites
items.summary:
It will provide the tools and channels to report, patch, and disclose open source software vulnerabilities.
The post Linux Foundation Unveils New Open Source Security Project Akrites appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Miasma Malware Targets npm Packages and GitHub Actions in Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the supply chain attack linked to the Mini Shai-Hulud, Miasma, and Hades malware family that has compromised a new set of npm packages, even as it has propagated to the Go ecosystem.

"The latest activity includes malicious npm releases

items.title: China-Linked Hackers Strike Asian Critical Infrastructure with TinyRCT Backdoor
items.summary: A China-linked threat group has been targeting critical infrastructure in Southeast Asia with a new custom backdoor called TinyRCT

items.title: $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack
items.summary:
The decentralized prediction market said hackers targeted some of its users through a compromise of a third-party vendor.
The post $3 Million Reportedly Stolen in Polymarket Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Warns of Photo ZIP Phishing Campaign Targeting Hotels with Node.js Implant
items.summary:
An active phishing campaign has been targeting hotel and other hospitality organizations across Europe and Asia since April 2026, using photo-themed ZIP files to drop a Node.js implant and dig into front-desk machines, Microsoft says.

The company has not attributed the activity to a known

items.title: Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets
items.summary:
Turla has been using the backdoor against government and military organizations in Ukraine for espionage.
The post Russian APT Deploys ‘StockStay’ Backdoor Against Ukrainian Targets appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russia Used Cellebrite on Jailed Activist's iPhone Months After Sales Cutoff
items.summary:
Russian authorities used Cellebrite's UFED forensic tools to break into the iPhone of detained opposition activist Andrey Pivovarov in June 2021, three months after Cellebrite said it would stop selling its tools and services to Russia and Belarus.

The finding, published June 25 by the Citizen

items.summary:
A major overhaul of the Model Context Protocol shifts critical security responsibilities from the protocol itself to developers and platform operators.
The post New Enterprise-Ready MCP Specification Brings New Security Challenges appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CMC Releases Analysis and Guidance for Education Sector After Canvas Data Breach
items.summary: The UK Cyber Monitoring Centre reviews the Canvas breach affecting 160 UK universities, highlighting data theft risks and financial impacts of cyber incidents

items.title: Philip Martin Joins Uber as Chief Information Security Officer
items.summary:
Martin brings experience from Coinbase, Palantir, Amazon, and the U.S. Army to lead Uber's cybersecurity and enterprise security organization.
The post Philip Martin Joins Uber as Chief Information Security Officer appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
items.summary: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone. [...]

items.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
items.summary: The fintech company's engineering-first application security team re-engineered the process for granting system access, making it easier and more secure for developers working on their projects. Here are the lessons learned from Robinhood's experience.

items.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
items.summary: Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks. [...]

items.title: CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure
items.summary:
Government entities and critical infrastructure were targeted for espionage in SE Asia by attackers using a hybrid toolkit, including custom TinyRCT backdoor. 
The post CL-STA-1062 Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Critical Infrastructure appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: In Less Than 24 Hours, Attackers Weaponize Cisco CUCM Flaw
items.summary: The flaw enables server-side request forgery (SSRF) and escalates privileges to root, impacting Cisco Unified CM and Unified CM SME deployments.

items.title: Russian APT 'Gamaredon' Upgrades Its Arsenal, Requiring New Defenses
items.summary: The FSB state-sponsored operation has gotten a lot better at loading its malware and hiding its servers.

items.title: EdTech Attackers Shift From Schools to Their Software Suppliers
items.summary: Educational institutions, the edtech companies they rely on, and, more concerningly, the challenges they pose for schools are the focus of the latest Reporters' Notebook video series.

items.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
items.summary: Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software. [...]

items.title: Local Police Collusion Hampers Crackdown on Asian Scam Centers
items.summary: With tens of billions of dollars flowing into regional economies from cybercrime, scam centers continue to flourish, despite international and law-enforcement efforts.

items.title: DHS chief says president has met with likely CISA nominee; agency plans to hire 600
items.summary: Once a new CISA director is in place, the agency will ramp up hiring efforts, Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin told lawmakers. The White House has not yet announced a nominee.

items.title: Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
items.summary: Microsoft has quietly extended its free Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program for consumers by an additional year, allowing enrolled devices to continue receiving security updates until October 12, 2027. [...]

items.title: Beyond IOCs: AI-enabled threat intelligence
items.summary: In this week’s newsletter, Martin considers how AI will help threat intelligence by creating an easily queryable data source of intelligence reports.

items.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
items.summary: A newly discovered macOS malware dubbed "Gaslight" is designed to confuse AI-assisted malware analysis tools by hiding prompt injection strings and fake debugging data within the executable. [...]

items.title: PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
items.summary: A major sports piracy ring linked to the illegal PirloTV streaming platform has been disrupted in an action that targeted 44 domains. [...]

items.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
items.summary: The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform continues to evolve with nearly 70 new hostnames identified over the past week and by adding browser-in-the-middle capabilities for improved data theft. [...]

items.title: Cisco Vulnerability Exploited Months Before Disclosure, Google Warns
items.summary: A high-severity flaw in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager disclosed in early June was exploited as early as March
